name: Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez
name_original: Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares desde Nava de Arévalo — circular por Pedro Rodríguez
region_intro: Hiking around Nava De Arévalo is characterized by its largely flat terrain, featuring extensive open fields and steppe lagoons that serve as vital habitats. The Arevalillo River flows through the region, providing some undulating areas within the otherwise level landscape. Nearby pine forests offer a contrasting natural environment to explore. This combination of features provides varied hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Nava De Arévalo The most popular hiking route is Arévalo River Walk, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This circular path offers scenic views along the riverside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route features gentle terrain and includes the Valtodano Picnic Area. Local hikers also love the Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Nava De Arévalo is defined by flat terrain, open fields, steppe lagoons, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more moderate routes. The routes in Nava De Arévalo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Nava De Arévalo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Nava de Arévalo?
A: There are over 8 hiking routes available in and around Nava de Arévalo, offering a variety of experiences from easy riverside strolls to more moderate treks through pine forests.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Nava de Arévalo?
A: Yes, Nava de Arévalo offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt; is an accessible 4.1 km circular path along the riverside, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256166544/arevalo-to-fuente-el-sauz-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail&lt;/a&gt; is another easy 4.8 km option featuring gentle terrain and the Valtodano Picnic Area.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Nava de Arévalo area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy circular trail. For a more moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248233708/ermita-del-cristo-de-los-pinares-from-nava-de-arevalo-circular-by-pedro-rodriguez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 12.5 km loop through pine forests. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146324807/from-tinosillos-to-el-bohodon-circular-by-the-camino-de-el-chorrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Tiñosillos to El Bohodón — circular by the Camino de El Chorrillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I explore while hiking near Nava de Arévalo?
A: The region is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, explore the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5856915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Villa and Church of San Martín&lt;/a&gt; in Arévalo, or admire the 16th-century Mudejar-style &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5987624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro Apóstol&lt;/a&gt; in Nava de Arévalo. The &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nava_de_Ar%C3%A9valo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arevalillo Riverbank&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views and opportunities for wildlife spotting.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Nava de Arévalo?
A: The climate in Nava de Arévalo, characterized by low winter temperatures and mild to moderate summers, makes it generally pleasant for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer particularly comfortable temperatures for hiking, while summer mornings can also be enjoyable.

Q: Are there any trails that go through the pine forests mentioned in the region?
A: Yes, the pine forests of Arévalo are a distinctive feature of the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248233708/ermita-del-cristo-de-los-pinares-from-nava-de-arevalo-circular-by-pedro-rodriguez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo&lt;/a&gt; trail leads through these forests, offering a different natural environment compared to the open fields.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Nava de Arévalo?
A: The hiking routes in Nava de Arévalo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from easy riverside paths to more moderate routes, and the combination of open fields, steppe lagoons, and pine forests that provide diverse scenery.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the trails?
A: Yes, the steppe lagoons and open fields around Nava de Arévalo are vital habitats and breeding grounds for various bird species. Hiking along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5768284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arevalillo Riverbank&lt;/a&gt; or near the lagoons can offer chances to observe local wildlife.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Nava de Arévalo?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not always listed, routes originating from towns like Arévalo or Nava de Arévalo typically have public parking options nearby. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Nava de Arévalo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in rural areas like Nava de Arévalo, especially on public paths. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a longer, moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248233708/ermita-del-cristo-de-los-pinares-from-nava-de-arevalo-circular-by-pedro-rodriguez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.5 km circular hike that requires good fitness and takes approximately 3 hours and 11 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in this region?
A: The region around Nava de Arévalo is characterized by largely flat terrain with extensive open fields and steppe lagoons. The Arevalillo River provides some undulating areas, and the nearby 'pinares de Arévalo' (pine forests) offer a contrasting environment. You'll experience a blend of open vistas and wooded sections.
tips:
This hermitage hosts one of the most massive pilgrimages in the province of Ávila, which is held every year on September 14.
With pleasant grassy areas around it, it is possible to stop for a picnic.

This church constitutes the most outstanding and relevant monument of the town. Romanesque in style, it is approximately eight centuries old. Its most outstanding characteristic is the Mudejar head, which presents a unique archway that originates from a brick plinth, accompanied by a curb course that culminates with corners and several superimposed curbs.

Likewise, the altarpiece located in the main chapel, which dates from the 16th century, deserves to be highlighted. This altarpiece is organized into three streets divided by inter-streets, and has four bodies that house various images of Saints and Biblical scenes. In total, it is made up of 18 paintings of great artistic value.
